name: module-scaffolder
description: Scaffolds new feature modules in DevPrep AI following the 6-folder architecture with proper TypeScript interfaces, path aliases, and quality standards. Use when creating new domains like 'analytics', 'notifications', or any new feature module.
capabilities: Scaffolds new feature modules following 6-folder architecture
Generates TypeScript components with proper I-prefixed interfaces
Creates barrel exports (index.ts) for clean imports
Validates module structure against quality standards
Supports adding components to existing modules
author: ArieGoldkin
securitySummary: All 418 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate module scaffolding tool using bash scripts and template substitution. The static analyzer misidentified documentation examples, relative imports, and template placeholders as security issues.
valueStatement: Manually creating feature modules requires following complex architecture patterns and naming conventions. This skill automates module creation with proper TypeScript interfaces, barrel exports, and quality standards enforced through validation.
userTitle: Create feature modules with standardized structure
limitations: Designed for DevPrep AI project structure specifically
Module names must use lowercase-with-hyphens format
Does not modify existing files beyond barrel exports

useCases.title: Add new feature module
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold a complete feature module with components, hooks, and types following project conventions.

useCases.title: Enforce architecture standards
useCases.description: Ensure all new modules follow the same structure with consistent naming and quality checks.

useCases.title: Learn project patterns
useCases.description: Reference the generated module structure and examples to understand project conventions.

promptTemplates.title: Basic module creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new module called analytics with the module-scaffolder skill

promptTemplates.title: Module with components
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a component called AnalyticsDashboard to the analytics module

promptTemplates.title: Validate structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate that the user-profile module follows all quality standards

promptTemplates.title: Generate reference module
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete-module as a reference example for the team

bestPractices: Plan module scope before scaffolding using the brainstorming skill
Use descriptive module names that reflect business domain
Run validation after scaffolding to ensure quality compliance
antiPatterns: Scaffolding modules with non-standard names breaks validation
Skipping validation leads to inconsistent architecture
Modifying generated templates without understanding impact

faq.question: What naming formats are required?
faq.answer: Module names use lowercase-with-hyphens. Component names use PascalCase. Interfaces use I prefix.

faq.question: Where are modules created?
faq.answer: Modules are created at frontend/src/modules/<module-name> following the 6-folder architecture.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates?
faq.answer: Yes, edit templates in templates/ directory to change generated code patterns.

faq.question: What validation checks are performed?
faq.answer: Validation checks directory structure, file size limits, I-prefix interfaces, no 'any' types, and import patterns.

faq.question: How do I delete a module?
faq.answer: This skill creates modules only. To remove, delete the module directory manually and update imports.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: Uses Read, Write, Bash, and Glob tools for file operations and script execution.
